executive_summary.problem_statement: As AI agent systems grow in complexity with multiple specialized agents working together, developers lack visual tools to understand agent team structures, dependencies, and configurations. Navigating YAML specifications manually is error-prone and makes it difficult to comprehend the overall system architecture.
executive_summary.proposed_solution: A visual workflow designer that renders multi-agent team specifications as interactive graph visualizations, allowing developers to explore agent relationships, inspect configurations, and manage custom views of their agent architectures.
expected_outcomes: Reduce time to understand agent team structures by 80%
Enable visual debugging of agent dependencies and configurations
Provide persistent views for different analysis perspectives
Support hot-reload for rapid iteration on agent specifications
executive_summary.target_audience: Developers and architects building multi-agent AI systems who need to visualize, understand, and manage complex agent team specifications
executive_summary.value_proposition: Transform complex agent YAML specifications into intuitive visual workflows that accelerate understanding and reduce configuration errors

business_objectives.description: Enable developers to quickly onboard to existing agent team codebases
business_objectives.rationale: Visual understanding accelerates developer productivity and reduces ramp-up time

business_objectives.description: Reduce agent configuration errors through visual validation
business_objectives.rationale: Seeing dependencies and relationships helps catch misconfigurations early

product_goals.description: Render agent teams as interactive node-based graphs
product_goals.rationale: Graph visualization is the natural representation for agent dependencies

product_goals.description: Provide detailed agent inspection via floating panels
product_goals.rationale: Developers need to see full agent configurations without losing graph context
Comparing teams side-by-side aids architecture decisions

product_goals.description: Persist UI layouts as named views
product_goals.rationale: Different tasks require different perspectives; views enable quick switching

success_metrics.name: Time to Understanding
success_metrics.description: Time for a new developer to understand an agent team structure

success_metrics.name: Spec Reload Latency
success_metrics.description: Time from file save to UI update

personas.name: Agent Developer
personas.description: Builds and maintains multi-agent AI systems, writes agent specifications, and debugs agent interactions
goals: Quickly understand existing agent team architectures
Identify agent dependencies and potential bottlenecks
pain_points: Reading YAML files doesn't convey the big picture
Hard to trace dependencies across multiple files
No visual feedback when modifying specifications

personas.name: System Architect
personas.description: Designs multi-agent system architectures, reviews team structures, and ensures best practices
goals: Review agent team designs for architectural soundness
Document and share system views with stakeholders
pain_points: Difficult to get a holistic view of complex agent systems
No standard way to visualize agent architectures for review
Manual diagramming is time-consuming and gets out of sync

user_stories.title: View Agent Team Graph
user_stories.i_want: to see my agent team rendered as an interactive graph
user_stories.so_that: I can understand the team structure at a glance

acceptance_criteria.description: Agents displayed as nodes with name and model type
acceptance_criteria.when: The team is loaded in the UI
acceptance_criteria.then: Each agent appears as a node with its name and model indicator
Edges connect dependent agents to their dependencies

user_stories.title: Inspect Agent Details
user_stories.i_want: to click on an agent node and see its full configuration
user_stories.so_that: I can review tools, instructions, and settings without leaving the graph view

acceptance_criteria.description: Floating panel opens on node click
acceptance_criteria.given: An agent node in the graph
acceptance_criteria.then: A floating panel opens showing agent details
Panel displays name, model, tools, dependencies, and instructions

user_stories.title: Multiple Floating Panels
user_stories.i_want: to open multiple agent panels simultaneously
user_stories.so_that: I can compare configurations between agents

acceptance_criteria.description: Multiple panels can be open at once
acceptance_criteria.then: A second panel opens without closing the first
Each new panel is offset from previous panels

user_stories.title: Multi-Team View
user_stories.i_want: to view multiple agent teams side by side
user_stories.so_that: I can compare team structures and identify patterns

acceptance_criteria.then: Teams appear in a 2x2 grid layout

user_stories.title: Save UI Layout
user_stories.i_want: to save my current UI layout as a named view
user_stories.so_that: I can quickly restore it later for specific tasks

acceptance_criteria.when: User saves the view with a name
acceptance_criteria.then: The view is persisted to the database
Selected teams, node positions, and sidebar width are restored

user_stories.title: Hot Reload Specifications
user_stories.i_want: the UI to update automatically when I edit spec files
user_stories.so_that: I can iterate on agent configurations rapidly

acceptance_criteria.given: The app is running and displaying a team
acceptance_criteria.when: User edits and saves a spec file
acceptance_criteria.then: The UI updates within 500ms without manual refresh

user_stories.title: Model Type Indicators
user_stories.i_want: to see visual indicators of which model each agent uses
user_stories.so_that: I can quickly identify agent capabilities and costs

acceptance_criteria.then: Headers are colored: opus=red, sonnet=blue, haiku=green

user_stories.title: Markdown Instructions Rendering
user_stories.i_want: agent instructions rendered as formatted markdown
user_stories.so_that: I can read complex instructions with proper formatting and syntax highlighting

acceptance_criteria.when: Instructions are displayed in a panel
acceptance_criteria.then: Markdown is rendered with headers, lists, and code blocks highlighted

functional.title: Team Discovery
functional.description: Automatically discover agent teams from workspace directories containing team.yaml files

functional.title: Graph Rendering
functional.description: Render agent teams as interactive node-edge graphs using React Flow

functional.title: Floating Panels
functional.description: Display agent details in draggable, resizable, minimizable floating panels

functional.title: View Persistence
functional.description: Store and retrieve named UI layouts via REST API backed by SQLite or PostgreSQL

functional.title: File Watching
functional.description: Monitor spec directories for changes and trigger UI updates

functional.title: Embedded Frontend
functional.description: Embed compiled frontend assets in the Go binary for single-file distribution

non_functional.title: Hot Reload Latency
non_functional.description: UI must update within 500ms of file changes

non_functional.title: Initial Load Time
non_functional.description: Application should be ready to use within 2 seconds of launch

non_functional.title: Team Size
non_functional.description: Support teams with up to 50 agents without performance degradation

non_functional.title: Cross-Platform
non_functional.description: Run on macOS, Linux, and Windows

milestones.name: v0.1.0 Release
deliverables: React Flow canvas with agent nodes

assumptions.description: Agent specifications follow a consistent YAML format with team.yaml and agents/*.yaml structure
assumptions.rationale: The tool is designed for a specific spec format used in the agentplexus ecosystem

assumptions.description: Users have Go and Node.js installed for development builds
assumptions.rationale: Standard toolchain for the tech stack

constraints.description: Must work offline without internet connectivity
constraints.rationale: Developers may work in air-gapped environments
